title: Market Brief — Saturday, June 20, 2026
summary: GPU compute is loose across every class. Util-avail sits at 39% for A100, 36% for H100, 44% for H200, and 43% for B200, all comfortably above oversupply thresholds after a brutal 30-day demand collapse of 28 to 39 percentage points in utilization. H100 spot ticked up 3.1% day-over-day to $2.27/hr, a dead-cat bounce against the structural glut, while H200 volatility at 158% annualized screams unstable price discovery.
paragraphs: GPU compute is loose across every class. Util-avail sits at 39% for A100, 36% for H100, 44% for H200, and 43% for B200, all comfortably above oversupply thresholds after a brutal 30-day demand collapse of 28 to 39 percentage points in utilization. H100 spot ticked up 3.1% day-over-day to $2.27/hr, a dead-cat bounce against the structural glut, while H200 volatility at 158% annualized screams unstable price discovery.

B200 is the only GPU where mid-tier inference providers break even, with TCO-adjusted cost of $4.00/M tokens enabling 47% margin at high-tier pricing. On A100 and H100 hardware, anyone selling Llama 70B output below $6/M is burning cash. DeepSeek at $0.34/M output on V3.2 and Meta Llama 3.3 70B hosts at $0.32/M are selling far below the $4-8 TCO-adjusted production cost, losing roughly $3 to $6 per million tokens. Grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

H200 at $3.21/hr spot with 44% availability and 158% vol is overvalued, a repricing lower is inevitable as utilization keeps sliding. B200 at $4.38/hr with the lowest inference cost per token and 62% annualized vol is the cleanest risk-adjusted long in the GPU stack.
bullets: 44% util-avail on H200 marks the loosest supply among premium GPU classes.
36% util-avail on H100 reflects a 28 percentage-point utilization drop in 30 days.
$0.18/M output from OpenAI gpt-oss-120b is the cheapest frontier-adjacent token on the board.
158% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is 2.5x the A100 rate, signaling a dislocated market.
21.78 on the LLMflation index means GPT-4 equivalent output costs 78% less than at March 2023 launch.
$80/M output for o3 Pro makes it 235x pricier than gpt-oss-120b from the same provider.
